steps.title: Set up
steps.content:
Read two points on line $k$ from the graph: $(-5,\ 1)$ and $(1,\ -4)$. Compute its slope:
$$m_k = \frac{-4-1}{1-(-5)} = \frac{-5}{6}$$

steps.title: Solve
steps.content:
Perpendicular lines have slopes that are negative reciprocals of each other:
$$m_j = -\frac{1}{m_k} = -\frac{1}{-5/6} = \frac{6}{5}$$
